decisions.summary: Created 3 beads: validate issueId in workspaces.ts, replace execAsync with execFileAsync in vulnerable functions, audit issues.ts and agents.ts
Used Python script to auto-insert parseIssueId() validation after all 32 params['issueId'] extractions in workspaces.ts. TypeScript typecheck passes.

Replaced execAsync shell string interpolation with execFileAsync argument arrays in getContainerStatusAsync (docker ps + JS grep), getMrUrlAsync (gh pr view), and ensurePRExists (gh pr view + gh pr create). TypeScript typecheck passes.

Audited issues.ts and added parseIssueId() validation to 21 unvalidated routes. Audited agents.ts — params['id'] is agent ID (system-generated), and buildTmuxCommandString already shell-quotes arguments, so no injection risk. TypeScript typecheck passes.

hazards.summary: 29 route handlers in workspaces.ts extract issueId without validation — CSRF via GET routes can trigger shell injection
hazards.mitigation: Add parseIssueId() at every extraction point; return 400 for invalid IDs

hazards.summary: execFileAsync in getContainerStatusAsync runs docker ps without shell grep pipe — need JS-side filtering to preserve behavior

resumePoint.description: All 3 beads complete. Remaining: run full test suite, commit, push, and call pan done.
